title: Goniopteris urbani

This species belongs to the genus Goniopteris, which is a group of ferns that are known for their distinctive leaf morphology. Goniopteris urbani is a type of vascular plant, and as such, it is classified within the kingdom Plantae. At the phylum level, Goniopteris urbani is classified as a member of the phylum Pteridophyta, which is a group of vascular plants that produce spores. The species is also classified as a member of the division Pteridophyta, which is a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores.

intro: Goniopteris urbani, also known as the Urban Tree Fern, is a species of fern native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Goniopteris urbani include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Goniopteris urbani is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and is also used in the form of a tincture, allowing for the extraction and utilization of its bioactive compounds.
